pairs.why: Both specify a natural adult ~1:7 head-to-body proportion (seven heads tall), expressing the same corrective intent despite the candidate's added geometric detail.
Both specify natural adult ~1:7 head-to-body proportions, so either clause steers the re-generation toward the same seven-heads-tall fix.
Both specify a natural ~7-head-tall adult proportion, so either clause steers the regeneration toward the same 1:7 fix despite the candidate's added precision.
Both steer the re-generation toward the identical canonical 1:7 head-to-body proportion; the candidate just adds landmark detail consistent with that same target.
Both specify the canonical 1:7 head-to-body proportion; the candidate just adds supporting landmark detail that elaborates the same corrective intent.
The candidate steers toward a three-quarter view with both eyes visible and explicitly forbids profile, while the golden reference calls for a left profile — opposite corrective intents.
The candidate steers toward a strict symmetric front view while the golden fix calls for a three-quarter view — opposite corrective intents.

The candidate describes a LEFT profile (left side of head to camera, nose toward screen-left), which is the mirror opposite of the reference's right profile (right side shown, nose toward screen-right).
Candidate steers to a pure directly-behind view with zero facial features, while the golden asks for a three-quarter back angle that still reveals some side-of-face — different target orientations.

Candidate steers to a ~45° three-quarter with both eyes fully readable and far cheek past the nose, while the golden targets ~60° between three-quarter and profile (closer to profile) — a meaningfully different view angle.
Both clauses correct the anatomy-count defect by enforcing exactly five fingers/digits per hand, so either would steer the re-generation toward the same fix.
Both correct the hand to show five distinct, individually drawn fingers, fixing the anatomy-count defect with the same intent.
no diff proposed

Both steer the re-generation toward the muted reference pencil-test palette with low/desaturated chroma, the same corrective intent despite the candidate's added color-spec detail.

Both steer the hair toward dirty-blonde/light-brown rather than darker tones, the same palette correction despite the candidate's added detail.

Both replace smooth digital shading with visible cross-hatch pencil/graphite shadows, the same shading-register correction despite the candidate's extra construction-line detail.
